definition: The blocker is a script-specific switch case arm for a particular Unicode codepoint inside a per-glyph shaper dispatch loop; context-sensitive coverage makes the target case arm a fresh coverage bucket under the shaper call path, providing the corpus retention gradient that drives havoc to refine glyph bytes toward the target codepoint.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the Oriya switch arm for codepoint 0x0B05 a fresh (call-context, edge) bucket so even a single visit under the shaper dispatch path earns new coverage, rewarding seeds with bytes 05 0b that plain edge coverage already considers saturated.
signature.one_line: context bucket makes 0x0B05 switch arm fresh coverage under Oriya shaper dispatch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must place the Oriya codepoint 0x0B05 (bytes 05 0b) at the current glyph slot to take the case 0x0B05u true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A glyph whose codepoint equals 0x0B05 must reach buffer->cur() inside the HB_SCRIPT_ORIYA arm; bytes 05 0b (little-endian 0x0B05) at the leading codepoint slot, sitting on the same Oriya call-context that the surrounding indic shaper exercises.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 trials. Winning seeds open with `05 0b 00 00` (e.g. seed cdda5341, fuzzer=naive_ctx), placing 0x0B05 at the current glyph. The CtxHook folds the Oriya call-context into the edge-map slot so even a single visit to case 0x0B05u becomes a brand-new (call-context, edge) bucket, rewarding seeds that drive the matched-then-_output_with_dotted_circle path.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10. Its loser seeds carry 0x0B0F (`0f 0b ...`) or noise (`c8 20 0b 0b`, `15 bd cb cb`) at the codepoint slot, landing on the sister case 0x0B0Fu/0x0B13u line 199 (L=2 true) instead of 0x0B05u (L=0 true). Without context-indexing, naive sees the bare ORIYA edges as already-covered and stops paying energy to refine toward 0x0B05.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook makes coverage context-sensitive: the same case-0x0B05u edge counts as new coverage per distinct call context, so the shaper-dispatch path that reaches it is rewarded and retained where plain edge coverage saturates.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the Oriya switch case comparing the current glyph codepoint to 0x0B05.
Two decisive pairs win over naive via different techniques: ctx_coverage and value_profile.
value_profile is the second decisive delta over naive.
naive_ctx resolves all 10 trials; naive blocks 8.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=2/10  blocked=8  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolves 8/10 trials where naive blocks 8.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=8/10  blocked=2  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=2/10  blocked=8  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: A winning seed stored by naive_ctx opens with bytes 05 0b 00 00, i.e. codepoint 0x0B05.
The winning naive_ctx seed's leading bytes are 05 0b 00 00.
Winning seeds carrying 0x0B05 are stored by fuzzer=value_profile.
A value_profile winning seed contains the bytes 05 0b at the codepoint slot.
Loser-blocking seeds open with 0f 0b, the sister case 0x0B0F codepoint, not 0x0B05.
The sister case 0x0B0Fu/0x0B13u sits at line 199, the false-side alternative reached by loser seeds.
Winner seeds hit the case 0x0B05u line and losers do not (L explores 0x0B0F instead).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 196  T=28 F=182  T=0 F=150  case 0x0B05u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker line is in the winner-only [W] overlay region while the sister case 0x0B0F is loser-only [L].
naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call context into the edge-map index, making (call-context, edge) pairs new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile writes integer-CMP operand-distance buckets into a CMP_MAP, preserving inputs whose operands are closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only per-edge hit-count bucket feedback and no I2S, so near-miss codepoints yield no new signal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile winner has nonzero branch hits while naive loser has zero.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1  loser=0

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the case 0x0B05u dispatch where naive resolves the true side as fast as naive_ctx and value_profile (no divergence), showing context-indexing and CMP_MAP gradient confer no advantage on this exact-codepoint gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed-byte evidence that value_profile (not just naive_ctx) drives the 0x0B05 match is correlational — the value_profile seeds also already contain 05 0b but the CMP_MAP gradient step that produced them is inferred from mechanism description, not observed in lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds call-context into the edge-map so the deeply nested Sinhala per-glyph switch arm registers as fresh (call-context, edge) coverage that bare naive already considers seen, driving the corpus toward the 0x0D85 codepoint (bytes 85 0d) across 10/10 trials.
signature.one_line: context bucket makes 0x0D85 Sinhala case arm fresh where bare edge coverage saturates

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must select Sinhala script and place a glyph with codepoint 0x0D85 (bytes 85 0d) so the per-script switch hits case 0x0D85u.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A buffer whose script resolves to HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA and that contains a glyph whose codepoint == 0x0D85 (little-endian bytes 85 0d) at a position where buffer->idx + 1 < count, so the inner per-glyph switch reaches the true side of case 0x0D85u at line 295.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the repeated 4-byte pattern 85 0d 00 00 (codepoint 0x0D85), and the per-role overlay marks line 295 case 0x0D85u as [W] winner-resolving only; the winner takes the true side 15 times (W T=15) at the blocker line.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry sibling Sinhala codepoints (91 0d = 0x0D91, ca 0d) but never 0x0D85, so the inner switch lands on case 0x0D91u (marked [B], both hit) and the blocker case 0x0D85u stays at T=0; the byte diff shows offset 0x0000 W=85 vs L=91/ca.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deeply nested per-glyph switch arm via the Sinhala for-loop registers as fresh (call-context, edge) coverage that bare naive's flat edge map already considers seen; this extra granularity rewards the corpus growth that drives the buffer toward a 0x0D85 glyph, resolving the branch in 10/10 trials vs 1/10 for naive.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of case 0x0D85u inside the HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA per-glyph switch.
The blocker case 0x0D85u sits inside the SINHALA script arm reached via the outer script switch.
The per-role overlay marks line 295 case 0x0D85u as winner-resolving only.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the codepoint 0x0D85 as little-endian bytes 85 0d 00 00.
Loser-blocking seeds carry sibling Sinhala codepoint 0x0D91 (bytes 91 0d) rather than 0x0D85.
At offset 0x0000 the winner bytes are dominated by 85 while losers carry 91/ca/4c/0c.
The winner takes the true side of the blocker 15 times while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 295  T=15 F=90  T=0 F=90  case 0x0D85u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser instead lands on the sibling Sinhala arm case 0x0D91u, which both roles hit.
naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different map slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx feedback treats a new (call-context, edge) pair as new coverage, distinguishing it from bare naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins the decisive pair 10/10 resolved vs naive 1/10, delta ctx_coverage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic Sinhala-script harness with a deeply nested per-glyph switch where naive and naive_ctx reach the 0x0D85 case-arm equally fast (no resolved-trial advantage for naive_ctx), showing the context-indexed coverage gave no exploration benefit.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link between context-sensitive coverage granularity and the corpus actually producing a 0x0D85 glyph is inferred; the prompt shows the byte/coverage correlation but not a direct trace of CtxHook-driven corpus adds leading to that codepoint.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map so the MODI-loop switch arm for 0x11600/0x11601 is a new (call-context, edge) bucket along the preprocess_text call chain, preserving seeds with bytes 16 01 that naive discards as already-covered.
signature.one_line: context bucket along preprocess_text chain enables Modi vowel 0x11600 case arm

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must drive a MODI-script buffer whose current glyph codepoint equals 0x11600 or 0x11601, reached via the multi-hop shaper call path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font/buffer input that selects HB_SCRIPT_MODI and presents a current-glyph codepoint of 0x11600u or 0x11601u (the leading Modi vowel) at the vowel-constraint scan position, so the true side of the case at line 432 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in 8/10 trials and its winner-resolving seeds carry the 16 01 byte run (e.g. seed 655f90fb at offset 0x0a: 6a 01 16 01) that encodes the 0x11600/0x11601 codepoint reaching the MODI case true side, where W shows T=5 on the blocker line.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 8/10 trials; its loser-blocking seeds reach the MODI switch (case 432 is [B]) but only fall through the false side (L shows T=0 F=75 on one blocker row) and never present the 0x11600/0x11601 codepoint at the scan position.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same MODI-loop edge counts as new coverage when reached via a different shaper call path; this finer (call-context, edge) granularity preserves intermediate seeds along the deep preprocess_text_use -> _hb_preprocess_text_vowel_constraints chain that plain naive's bare-edge feedback discards, letting it accumulate the codepoint that takes the true side.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx beating naive with delta ctx_coverage.
The blocked side is the true branch of the MODI codepoint case at line 432.
naive_ctx resolves the branch in 8 of 10 trials while naive resolves it in only 1.
The loser naive blocks the branch in 8 of 10 trials.
A winner-resolving naive_ctx seed carries the 16 01 byte run encoding the Modi codepoint at the scan position.
The winner takes the true side of the blocker line whereas the loser does not on that row.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 432  T=5 F=70  T=0 F=149  case 0x11600u: case 0x11601u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser reaches the MODI switch but the matched arm at line 435 is loser-only-blocking, never set true by the winner path needed.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots by call path.

keeps a running hash of the current call context (caller chain) and
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is reached through a multi-hop shaper call chain from preprocess_text_use.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic MODI-script harness in which naive (bare-edge) and naive_ctx reach the 0x11600/0x11601 true side at statistically indistinguishable rates would refute the claim that context-indexed coverage granularity is what enables the winner.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed-byte attribution of the 16 01 run to the 0x11600/0x11601 codepoint is inferred from offset coincidence, not from a confirmed font-parse dataflow trace into buffer->cur().codepoint.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook turns the Devanagari vowel-constraint edge into a fresh (call-context, edge) slot, rewarding scheduler energy on inputs carrying 0x0906+{0x093A/0x0945/0x0946/0x0947/0x0948} pairs; bare naive sees only an already-covered edge and never refines the second codepoint.
signature.one_line: context bucket rewards Devanagari 0x0906-plus-follower pair toward true switch arm

analysis.summary_one_line: A Devanagari buffer with codepoint U+0906 immediately followed by U+093A/0945/0946/0947/0948 to enter the matching vowel-constraint case-arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A glyph buffer whose script is HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI containing an adjacent pair: current codepoint 0x0906 (case at line 74) followed by next-glyph codepoint in {0x093A, 0x0945, 0x0946, 0x0947, 0x0948}, which makes the inner switch at line 75 take the true case-arm at line 77.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner-resolving seed carries repeated little-endian 0x0906 / 0x0945 codepoint words near offsets 0x10-0x21 (06 09 00 00 ... 45 09 00 00), supplying both the 0x0906 head glyph and a 0x0945-family follower; on the winner the blocker line 77 records T=1 (true side taken) versus the loser's T=0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds reach the 0x0906 case (line 74 F=28) and execute the inner switch, but the follower glyph never lands in {0x093A,0x0945,0x0946,0x0947,0x0948}, so line 77 stays at T=0 F=2 and the true branch is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same vowel-constraint edge reached via the Indic preprocess_text call path becomes a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket; this rewards the scheduler for keeping/mutating Devanagari inputs that drive deeper into the per-script switch, letting it hill-climb to the specific 0x0906+follower pair, whereas plain naive sees only a bare already-covered edge and gets no signal to refine the second codepoint.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case-arm at line 77 inside the 0x0906 inner switch, with the true side globally blocked.
Reaching the blocker requires the current glyph to be 0x0906 in the Devanagari arm.
The winner-resolving seed was stored by naive_ctx and contains repeated 0x0906 and 0x0945 little-endian codepoint words.
On the winner the blocker line 77 takes the true side (T=1) while the loser stays at T=0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L  77  T=1 F=1  T=0 F=2  case 0x093Au: case 0x0945u: case 0x0946u: case 0x0947u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser reaches the 0x0906 case but never satisfies the follower codepoint condition.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so a (call-context, edge) pair is new coverage.

folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx > naive with delta ctx_coverage and prob_div 0.90.
naive_ctx uses no I2S substitution and no CMP_MAP.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic Devanagari harness where adding the CtxHook (naive_ctx) does NOT improve the rate of taking the 0x0906-follower true arm over plain naive at matched budget, i.e. context-indexed coverage gives no measurable advantage in resolving the nested per-codepoint switch.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is available, so the byte-level evidence that 0x0906+0x0945 adjacency (not some other follower) is what flips line 77 is inferred from a single example rather than corroborated across seeds.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the deeply nested ORIYA per-codepoint case for 0x0B0F/0x0B13 a new (call-context, edge) bucket that bare naive's single slot never rewards, keeping seeds with bytes 0f 0b / 13 0b until the true case fires.
signature.one_line: context bucket for ORIYA case 0x0B0F/0x0B13 retains codepoint-matching seeds

analysis.summary_one_line: An Oriya-script glyph sequence whose current codepoint is 0x0B0F or 0x0B13 must appear in the buffer for the switch arm to be taken.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The decoded glyph buffer must (1) select the HB_SCRIPT_ORIYA switch arm and (2) at some loop position have a current codepoint equal to 0x0B0F or 0x0B13, i.e. the input must encode the exact little-endian glyph bytes 0F 0B or 13 0B at a 4-byte glyph slot so buffer->cur().codepoint hits that case label.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every naive_ctx winner seed is built from repeated 4-byte glyph records and several carry the literal 0f 0b / 13 0b codepoint pattern: winner seed 89dfff878e1186bc and 61a1738ca6c821b1 both begin '13 0b 00 00', placing 0x0B13 as a current glyph; the per-role overlay shows the winner takes line 199 TRUE 15 times (T=15 F=60) while the loser never does (T=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds reach the ORIYA loop but their glyph bytes never present 0x0B0F/0x0B13 as a current codepoint at line 199 (loser T=0 F=150 on the TRUE-deciding case row); their bytes are scattered high/ASCII values (e.g. cb cb cb, 72 61 66 'raf', 6f6f6f) so the codepoint match never fires and the matched flag stays false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller-chain hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deeply nested per-codepoint case inside the ORIYA loop under this specific call context registers as a NEW (call-context, edge) bucket that bare naive never distinguishes; this finer coverage granularity keeps the seeds that march the buffer index toward the 0x0B0F/0x0B13 codepoint, letting naive_ctx resolve the true side while naive sees no novelty and abandons it.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the ORIYA switch arm gated on current codepoint 0x0B0F or 0x0B13.
The blocked true side is only reachable inside the HB_SCRIPT_ORIYA loop.
The matched outcome additionally requires the next glyph to equal 0x0B57.
The winner takes the true side at line 199 (T=15) while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=15 F=60  T=0 F=150  case 0x0B0Fu: case 0x0B13u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner naive_ctx seeds carry the literal 13 0b codepoint bytes that place 0x0B13 as a current glyph.
Loser naive seeds contain scattered non-Oriya bytes rather than the required codepoint pattern.
The winner resolves all 10 trials while the loser is blocked all 10, defining the decisive ctx_coverage pair.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds call context into the edge-map index so a context+edge pair is treated as new coverage.

folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses only havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP comparison-solving.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive_ctx fails to resolve this branch any faster than bare naive even though the only nesting difference is the per-codepoint switch depth inside a script-selected loop, indicating context-sensitive coverage granularity is not what drives the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the finer (call-context, edge) granularity specifically rewards marching the buffer index toward 0x0B0F/0x0B13 is inferred from mechanism description plus seed bytes, not directly observed in lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the Sinhala line-303 switch arm (0x0D8B/0x0D8F/0x0D94) a fresh (call-context, edge) slot under the USE/Indic preprocess_text path, so the sibling 0x0D91 arm already hit by naive never satisfies the 0x0D8x target bucket, driving corpus toward 8f 0d / 8b 0d bytes.
signature.one_line: context bucket separates 0x0D8B/8F/94 Sinhala arm from already-hit sibling 0x0D91

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode a Sinhala-script buffer with codepoint 0x0D8B/0x0D8F/0x0D94 at the current glyph to take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input parsed into an HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA buffer whose current glyph codepoint equals one of 0x0D8B, 0x0D8F, or 0x0D94 (the line-303 switch arm), distinct from the 0x0D91 arm the loser settles into. Byte-level this means the little-endian codepoint bytes 8b/8f/94 followed by 0d appear at the parsed glyph offset.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner naive_ctx seeds carry the exact 8f 0d and 8d 0d little-endian Sinhala codepoint byte-pairs at parsed glyph offsets (e.g. seed 1 '8f 8f 8f 0d', seed 2 '8d 0d ... 8f 0d', seed 4 '8f 0d 00 00 8d 0d'); the per-role overlay marks line 303 [W] (winner-resolving only) and the divergent-branch table shows W taking the true side T=12 while L never does (T=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds reach the SINHALA switch but land on the 0x0D91 arm (line 309 marked [L]); e.g. loser seed 1 is the 2-byte '91 0d', i.e. codepoint 0x0D91, which routes to line 309 not line 303. The overlay marks lines 303-307 [W] and lines 309-316 [L], and the divergent-branch table shows L at line 303 with T=0 (true side never taken).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx differs from naive only by the CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same line-303 edge is a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket when reached via the USE/Indic preprocess_text call path. That extra granularity rewards a seed that drives the Sinhala arm, retaining and mutating it toward the 0x0D8B/0x0D8F/0x0D94 codepoints; plain naive collapses the edge to a single bare-edge bucket already satisfied by the sibling 0x0D91 arm, so it never gets corpus pressure to discover line 303.

evidence_trail.claim: The only decisive pair at this branch is naive_ctx > naive with technique delta ctx_coverage, prob_div 1.00.
The winner resolved all 10 trials and the loser blocked all 10, a clean prob_div=1.00 separation.
The blocker is the Sinhala switch arm selecting codepoints 0x0D8B/0x0D8F/0x0D94.
The line-303 arm is hit only by winner-resolving seeds in the per-role overlay (tag [W]).
Loser-blocking seeds instead reach the sibling 0x0D91 switch arm (tag [L]).
At line 303 the winner takes the true side 12 times while the loser never takes it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 303  T=12 F=93  T=0 F=90  case 0x0D8Bu: case 0x0D8Fu: case 0x0D94u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: A loser seed is the 2-byte input 91 0d, i.e. little-endian codepoint 0x0D91, routing to the 0x0D91 arm not line 303.
A winner seed carries the little-endian Sinhala codepoint bytes 8f and 0d (0x0D8F) and 8d 0d (0x0D8D) at parsed glyph offsets.
naive_ctx differs from naive only by a CtxHook that makes a (call-context, edge) pair count as new coverage.

a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses no I2S or CMP_MAP, only the havoc+token stack, so the divergence is purely the coverage-granularity feedback.

naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a single-context (no shared caller chain) switch on the same codepoint set in which naive_ctx shows no advantage over naive at reaching the 0x0D8B/0x0D8F/0x0D94 arm — i.e. removing call-context multiplicity eliminates the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-to-glyph mapping is inferred from the harfbuzz fuzzer input encoding rather than shown directly; I assume the 8f/8d/94 0d byte-pairs in winner seeds are parsed into the Sinhala codepoints that drive line 303.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook records the vowel-constraints edge at separate (call-context, edge) slots per shaper call path (preprocess_text_use vs preprocess_text_indic), retaining seeds carrying bytes 91 0d where bare-edge naive collapses them to one already-seen slot.
signature.one_line: per-shaper-path context buckets for 0x0D91 Sinhala arm drive corpus retention

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach the Sinhala vowel-constraint switch and present codepoint U+0D91 as the current glyph so case 0x0D91u is selected.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same Sinhala-script buffer with current glyph U+0D91, but reached and rewarded because the CtxHook indexes the edge by call context, making the (caller-chain, edge) pair fresh coverage that survives into the corpus.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same vowel-constraints edges count as new coverage when reached through different shaper call paths (preprocess_text_use / preprocess_text_indic), giving extra corpus retention that drives it to resolve all 10 trials with 85 avg hits; its resolving seeds (naive_ctx seeds 1,4,5) carry the 91 0d codepoint and contextual byte runs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive collapses every reach of the edge to one map slot regardless of call context, so once the bare edge is seen there is no new-coverage incentive to refine inputs toward the 0x0D91 arm; it blocks for the full 16.00h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook context-sensitive coverage records (call-context, edge) pairs as new buckets, retaining more intermediate seeds along the shaper call chain and thereby exploring the Sinhala switch arms that bare-edge naive abandons.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case 0x0D91u switch arm inside the Sinhala script block of the vowel-constraints preprocessor.
The arm is selected by an equality switch on the current glyph codepoint.
Pair 1 is value_profile beating naive with the value_profile delta.
value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive is blocked in all 10.
value_profile uses a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance bucket as a proximity gradient.

each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

evidence_trail.claim: An input producing a closer CMP operand pair is preserved as corpus, supplying the hill-climbing gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a bare-equality switch arm on a 16-bit codepoint where value_profile fails to resolve faster than naive (no CMP_MAP proximity advantage), and naive_ctx fails to resolve faster than naive when only call-context differs.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact mapping from input bytes through the harfbuzz harness to a Sinhala-script buffer with U+0D91 as the current glyph is inferred from the literal 91 0d byte runs and source switch, not directly observed at the codepoint level.
